behavior_surfaces: Desktop chat user messages submitted while a session is active are queued locally instead of being sent immediately.
Desktop composer primary action shows Stop only when the active session is running and the composer has no payload; typing a new payload switches the action to Run for queued submission.
Queued desktop user messages are marked pending/queued in UI state and submitted on the next message_complete boundary.
Queued messages render in a dedicated ChatInput composer queue instead of the MessageList transcript.
Queued message Guide action promotes the selected queued message to the front of the local queue without interrupting the active turn.
Queued message Delete action removes the selected queued message from both visible UI state and the outbound queue.
A stopped session's status-idle acknowledgement drains exactly the next queued message, honoring Guide priority.
state_contracts: Idle non-team sessions still send user_message over WebSocket immediately.
Non-idle non-team sessions keep the active turn state intact and enqueue the outbound payload until message_complete sets the session idle.
Guide only changes local queued message priority; it does not call wsManager.send while the session is busy.
Guide during local stop-pending state still only changes queue priority; the next queued prompt waits for the server status-idle acknowledgement before sending.
Delete removes only queued pending user_text messages and never mutates already-submitted transcript turns.
When chatState is non-idle and the composer has text or non-team attachments/context, the primary action calls handleSubmit instead of stopGeneration.
Queued outbound payloads are cleared when the session disconnects, is cleared, or is removed from the chat store.

confidence_notes: Live source reads confirmed desktop sendMessage previously sent user_message immediately even while chatState was non-idle.
Server and CLI code already serialize or queue downstream work; this change keeps the desktop UI and outbound WebSocket timing aligned with that behavior.
Tests prove Guide promotion does not send immediately and drains only at message_complete.
Tests prove Delete removes the queued outbound payload before the next message_complete drain.
Tests prove Stop/status-idle drains the guided queued prompt only after the server idle acknowledgement.
ChatInput test proves a running session with typed composer content shows Run, does not stop generation, and appends a queued user_text message.
MessageList excludes queued user messages from the transcript, while ChatInput owns the dedicated composer queue UI.
user_decisions: Do not use computer-use for implementation.
Guide must not interrupt the current turn; it should insert/promote for processing after the active tool/turn reaches a safe boundary.
Follow Codex App-style queued message behavior.

included: Desktop ChatInput running primary action state
Desktop queued user message composer rendering
Composer Guide and Delete action wiring
excluded: Stop/cancel queued-message semantics beyond existing stop behavior
